name: Main Impact
description_short: Main Impact Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Calloway County, Kentucky, offering deposit accounts, loans, and financial services to employees of local institutions and community residents.
description_long:
Main Impact Federal Credit Union was originally chartered in February 1978 to serve employees and faculty of Murray State University. Over the decades, it expanded its field of membership to include employees of Murray Independent School District (2000), Calloway County School District (2000), and Murray-Calloway County Hospital (2001). In 2023, the credit union was renamed from MSU Federal Credit Union to Main Impact Federal Credit Union, and in November 2023 completed a conversion to a Community Charter, opening membership to anyone living, working, worshiping, or attending school in Calloway County.

Main Impact offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including online banking, share accounts, loans, credit cards, and various account management tools. Members can access ATM machines, safety deposit boxes, notary services, and check reordering. The credit union provides wire transfer capabilities through the Volunteer Corporate Credit Union in Nashville and maintains a detailed fee schedule for services such as overdraft transfers, returned items, stop payment orders, and statement copies.

The credit union distinguishes itself as the longest-standing locally owned financial institution in Calloway County, emphasizing member-centric service with the philosophy that members are treated as friends rather than account numbers. Members have voting rights and ownership stakes in the organization. The credit union is planning expansion, with groundbreaking announced for a new location at 1312 Main Street, expected to open in late 2024.

As a federally chartered credit union, Main Impact provides NCUA insurance protection on member deposits. However, the website provides limited detail on loan products, interest rates, credit card terms, or specific account minimums. The credit union serves a defined regional market and may have membership field-of-membership restrictions despite recent conversion to community charter status.
services: Online banking with secure login access
Personal loans and loan calculator tools
Credit cards with rewards program (ScoreCard Rewards)
Wire transfers through Volunteer Corporate Credit Union
pros: Longest-standing locally owned financial institution in Calloway County with 45+ years of operations
Recently converted to community charter (November 2023), opening membership beyond original employer groups to all Calloway County residents
Member-owned structure with voting rights and non-profit status, meaning profits return to members
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal deposit protection
Comprehensive services including online banking, credit cards with rewards program, safety deposit boxes, and notary services
24/7 lost/stolen card reporting and after-hours customer service available
Toll-free customer service line during business hours (888-415-6154) with extended support
Planning new physical location at 1312 Main Street with anticipated late 2024 opening
cons: Limited online information about loan products, interest rates, terms, and eligibility requirements
Relatively small regional credit union serving only Calloway County, Kentucky area with limited branch presence
Website does not clearly explain specific membership requirements or confirm full community charter access despite conversion
No transparent display of APRs, loan limits, or account fee comparisons to help consumers make informed decisions
Moderate fees for services like NSF ($25), stop payment orders ($25), and garnishment ($20) compared to some larger institutions
best_for: Employees of Murray State University, Murray Independent School District, Calloway County School District, or Murray-Calloway County Hospital seeking member-owned banking
Calloway County residents wanting to support a local financial institution with 45+ year history
Members prioritizing community-focused banking with voting rights and ownership stakes over larger national credit unions
diagnosis: Main Impact is best for Calloway County residents—especially current and former employees of local schools, universities, and hospitals—seeking community-focused member-owned banking with local decision-making and voting rights. The primary caveat is limited online transparency regarding specific loan products, interest rates, and terms, requiring direct contact with the credit union for detailed product information before committing to membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Main Impact — Credit Union in Murray, KY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Main Impact — NCUA-insured credit union in Murray, KY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
